title: De la Espriella Says Colombia Will Seek to Refinance Debt
summary: Colombian President-elect Abelardo de la Espriella said his incoming administration will seek to refinance the nation’s debt to extend maturities and lower borrowing costs.

title: Giro hacia el libre mercado de Cuba no logra convencer a EE.UU.
summary: Cuba enfrenta dificultades para convencer al gobierno de Donald Trump de que sus reformas económicas más ambiciosas en décadas son suficientes para impulsar los cambios que exige Estados Unidos. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Fed's Daly Sees Inflation Slowing, Warns of Uncertainties
summary: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly says inflation should start to slow but the economic outlook remains uncertain during a Bank of Spain event in Santander, Spain. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Employment Picture ‘Just OK,’ BlackRock’s Rieder Says
summary: Rick Rieder, global fixed income CIO at BlackRock, says “hiring is stable, but I would say, broadly unimpressive,” as he examines the June US jobs report. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Adani Flagship Seeks $1 Billion Via Placement, Terms Show
summary: Adani Enterprises Ltd. is seeking to raise as much as 100 billion rupees ($1 billion) through a sale of shares to institutional investors as the ports-to-power conglomerate led by billionaire Gautam Adani overcomes legal challenges in the US.

title: CoreWeave Junk Bonds Drop as Investors Question AI Boom
summary: Bonds issued last month by AI infrastructure firm CoreWeave Inc. fell for a second day running on Meta Platforms Inc.’s plans for a competing cloud business.

title: India Plans to Expand Oil Reserves to Guard Against Price Spikes
summary: India plans to build larger oil inventories, expand storage capacity and deepen supply partnerships to hedge against the kind of price volatility seen during the Iran war.

title: Two Blue Owl Private Credit Funds Impose Caps After Redemption Requests
summary: For the second straight quarter, two Blue Owl Capital Inc. private credit funds were hit with the industry’s largest redemption requests, forcing the manager to again cap withdrawals.

title: Congo Plans First Stock Market as AI Mineral Boom Draws Interest
summary: The Democratic Republic of Congo is drawing up plans for its first stock exchange as it seeks to attract more investment to an economy that’s benefiting from soaring demand for minerals critical to the artificial intelligence buildout.

title: US Hiring Slows Sharply, Curbing Recent Job-Market Momentum
summary: US hiring slowed sharply in June even as the unemployment rate fell, curbing some of the budding momentum in job growth this year.

title: Here Are the Key Takeaways From the US Jobs Report for June
summary: Here are the key takeaways from the June US employment report released Thursday:

title: US Stocks Advance as Jobs Data Dims Prospect of Fed Rate Hike
summary: US stocks rose as slower-than-expected job growth drove traders to dial-back bets that the Federal Reserve could start raising interest rates as soon as this month.

title: Jobs Report Great for Warsh and Bonds, BlackRock's Rosenberg Says
summary: Jeffrey Rosenberg, portfolio manager of the systematic multi-strategy fund at BlackRock, says the June jobs report will help Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh be patient on rates as well as benefit the bond market. He speaks on "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Bond Investors Pull Back as Central America Rallies Go ‘Too Far’
summary: Investors are pulling back from a niche trade in Central American and Caribbean government bonds that delivered some of the best returns in emerging markets in recent years.

title: Stocks Rise as Jobs Fuel Bets Fed to Stay on Hold: Markets Wrap
summary: A slower-than-anticipated increase in US jobs drove stocks higher and short-dated bond yields fell on bets the Federal Reserve won’t be forced to raise interest rates any time soon.

title: Springbok Emblems May Get Protection Before All Blacks Visit
summary: South Africa proposed protecting the commercial rights to the Springboks’ upcoming rugby tour against New Zealand’s All Blacks, a move aimed at safeguarding sponsorship revenue.

title: Adani, IHC Sign MOU for $11.5 Billion India Aluminum Project
summary: Billionaire Gautam Adani’s flagship Adani Enterprises Ltd. and Abu Dhabi’s International Holding Co. plan to invest $11.5 billion in an aluminum project in the eastern state of Odisha, as the Indian conglomerate continues to build on its metal sector ambitions.

title: Treasuries Gain as Weak Jobs Report Dims Fed Rate-Hike Bets
summary: Treasuries rallied after a weaker-than-expected jobs report prompted traders to scale back expectations that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates in the coming months.

title: Saudi Oil Flows Hit 90% of Pre-War Rate as Ships Exit Hormuz
summary: Saudi Arabia’s crude oil exports have surged close to their pre-war levels as the kingdom starts getting its tankers through the Strait of Hormuz again, one of the most emphatic indications yet that Persian Gulf shipments are normalizing in the wake of an interim peace deal between the US and Iran.

title: BlackRock Partner Securitize Rides Wall Street Tokenization Wave
summary: Carlos Domingo spent years trying to persuade investors that stocks, bonds and private funds would one day live on blockchains — an idea that stayed largely confined to crypto’s fringes.

title: US Adds Fewer Jobs Than Expected in June, Unemployment Rate Falls
summary: US hiring slowed sharply in June as nonfarm payrolls increased 57,000 last month after downward revisions to the prior two months, according to Bureau of Labor Statistics data out Thursday. Michael McKee reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: India Food Watchdog Targets Energy Drinks Including Red Bull for Misbranding
summary: India’s food regulator has issued notices to at least six energy-drink makers including Red Bull GmbH’s local unit, for allegedly making misleading claims, tightening oversight of a rapidly expanding category in the country.

title: Oil Deepens Slide as Saudi Exports Reach 90% of Pre-War Levels
summary: Oil deepened its slide below pre-war levels as Saudi Arabia ramped up crude exports toward normal rates, extending the recovery in flows from the Persian Gulf and heightening the prospect of a supply surplus.

title: French Heat Wave May Have Slashed Corn Crop by Up to a Third
summary: A heat wave that swept across western Europe last week may have damaged almost a third of French corn, further worsening crop prospects in the region’s top grower.

title: Tech Rally Chasers Make Calls Priciest Since 2007 Next to S&P
summary: Normally, a three-month, 30% rally in the Nasdaq 100 Index would tempt investors to take the money and run. But right now, they’re betting the advance is just getting started.

title: Banks Get Bids on $49 Billion Paramount Debt Well Before Sale
summary: Banks have lined up enough investor demand to cover most of the roughly $49 billion debt package backing Paramount Skydance Corp.’s takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. — long before launching the deal.

title: Genneia Files for the First Argentine IPO in the US Since 2019
summary: Power producer Genneia SA filed for an initial public offering in New York, trying to become the first Argentine company to IPO in the US in at least seven years.

title: US Premarket Movers for July 2, 2026
summary: S&P 500 Index futures were little changed as of 7:53 a.m. New York time as investors rotate out of the high-flying momentum stocks that powered last quarter’s rally.

title: Leveraged ETFs Are Just the Tip of the Iceberg
summary: Record leverage is sweeping through the financial system, exposing hedge funds, banks, retail traders and money market funds to potential mounting losses.
